mission.title: envoy: Prometheus: add native histogram support
mission.description: Prometheus: add native histogram support. Requested by 27+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
*Title*: Prometheus: add native histogram support

*Description*:
https://prometheus.io/docs/concepts/metric_types/#histogram

Prometheus has a new native histogram mode which (claims to) make a much better tradeoff between metrics cost and

steps.title: Apply the fix for Prometheus: add native histogram support
steps.description:
There are a few steps that would be needed to implement this:

1) Add support in Envoy for the protobuf format for prometheus stats. It would probably need either a different path on the admin endpoint, or a query parameter/header to indicate this is what we want, or possibly infer it via content-type headers.

2) Possibly create a different histogram backend than the current `libcircllhist` one. I'm not sure if the output formats for native histograms and the storage in `libcircllhist` work

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Prometheus: add native histogram support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
There are a few steps that would be needed to implement this:

1) Add support in Envoy for the protobuf format for prometheus stats. It would probably need either a different path on the admin endpoint, or a query parameter/header to indicate this is what we want, or possibly infer it via content-type headers.

2) Possibly create a different histogram backend than the current `libcircllhist` one.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
